nodes.en: Music and songs are vocal and instrumental compositions integral to cultural expression, ritual, and social bonding. They transmit emotional and spiritual meaning across generations through performance.
nodes.cs: Hudba a písně jsou vokální a instrumentální kompozice integrální do kulturního vyjádření, rituálu a sociálního spojení. Přenášejí emocionální a spirituální smysl mezi generacemi prostřednictvím výkonu.
nodes.brief:
Music and songs are vocal and instrumental compositions — lullabies, work songs, ceremonial chants, ballads, dance music — that carry emotional and spiritual meaning through performance rather than through text alone. A song's melody, rhythm, and performance context are as much a part of its cultural content as its lyrics, which is why recording only the words of a traditional song loses most of what made it worth preserving.

Music is frequently the performative core of [[10.08]] Rituals & Ceremonies, providing the sonic structure that paces a rite; sung narrative overlaps with [[10.06]] Stories & Narratives wherever a ballad tells a story in musical form, and a song's lyrics survive only within the specific [[10.01]] Language in which they were composed, so translation for meaning necessarily loses the sound-meaning fit of the original. Where a song transmits historical memory rather than fiction or ritual, it functions as sung [[10.10]] Oral History.

Ethnomusicological archives preserve recordings alongside performance context: the [Smithsonian Folkways](https://folkways.si.edu/) collection documents traditional and folk music worldwide with liner notes on cultural context, and the [International Council for Traditional Music](https://ictmusic.org/) sets scholarly standards for ethnomusicological fieldwork and preservation.
